place: Ogdensburg borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Every first Tuesday of March, June, September, and December, the Ogdensburg Historical Society meets in the town’s historic museum.
The Old Schoolhouse and Firehouse Museum, built in 1910, now displays a scale model of the zinc mine hoist and other local memorabilia.
The Sterling Hill zinc mine, New Jersey’s last operating mine, closed in 1986 and now serves as a museum on the National Register of Historic Places.
Ogdensburg lies in the Wallkill Valley, covering 2.2 square miles east and west of Route 517, and is home to roughly 2,800 residents.
Heater’s Pond offers residents a pristine forested area perfect for swimming and sunbathing.
The museum’s first‑floor lobby houses a display case of minerals from the Ogdensburg Zinc Mine, originally located in the grade school.
